summary: A brief biographical overview of Walt Disney, highlighting his early life, his legacy of imagination and optimism, and his later focus on urban planning through the creation of EPCOT as a prototype community of tomorrow.

principles.rationale: When tackling massive challenges like urban decay, the foundation of any solution must be rooted in what the public actually requires rather than just applying superficial fixes.
principles.source_quote: Well, we’re convinced we must start with the public need.

principles.statement: Start from scratch to build the future.
principles.rationale: Merely patching up the problems of existing systems is insufficient; true progress requires building a new prototype on a blank slate.
principles.source_quote: We think the need is for starting from scratch on virgin land and building a community that will become a prototype for the future.

mental_models.name: The Prototype Community
mental_models.description: Treating urban development like industrial design by building a functional 'prototype' to test and showcase solutions for the future, rather than just theorizing.
mental_models.example: Designing EPCOT (Experimental Prototype Community of Tomorrow) on virgin land to solve the problems of modern cities.

signature_quotes.text: I don’t believe there is a challenge anywhere in the world that is more important to people everywhere than finding the solution to the problems of our cities.
signature_quotes.context: Speaking about his motivation for designing EPCOT.

signature_quotes.text: We think the need is for starting from scratch on virgin land and building a community that will become a prototype for the future.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining his approach to urban planning and the creation of EPCOT.

anti_patterns.description: Focusing solely on curing the old ills of old cities.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It limits innovation to patching existing, broken systems rather than designing a comprehensive, forward-looking prototype from the ground up.
